summary: Iter73 responsive audit (frontend-only). 51 route x viewport combos (17 routes x 3 viewports: 375/800/1280) — ZERO horizontal overflow anywhere (docWidth == innerWidth on every combo). Mobile 375px modal usability verified for: playbook builder (opens, pb-save 44px tall reachable at y=732, pb-live-preview present, no overflow), documents share modal (share-to input 303x38, doc-share-send 303x40, no overflow), email blast modal (blast-template populated, blast-send reachable, no overflow), header menu dropdown (288px wide, x=71 => fully on-screen), app-notif-sheet (bottom-sheet 375x556, no overflow), PDF preview (iframe + Download button both render). Tablet 800px: pipeline kanban scrolls INSIDE its own container (scrollWidth=2388 vs clientWidth=728) with page docWidth==800 — perfect. HR tab pills wrap cleanly (12 pills wrap across rows). Settings inputs render without clipping. Desktop 1280px: no regression from sm 640->600 breakpoint change. Two routes redirect gracefully: /app/hr-letters -> /app/hr (HR Letters tab), /app/email-templates -> /app/outreach (Templates tab); both render fine. /app/emailing loads OK. Zero unexpected console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: index.css RESPONSIVE HARDENING block — the z-index bottom-sheet override uses attribute-substring class matching (e.g. [class*='z-[6']) — works today but is brittle if Tailwind ever tree-shakes or renames the bracket-arbitrary classes; consider tagging modal roots with an explicit `data-mobile-sheet` attribute instead.

Tailwind screens change from sm:640 -> sm:600 is a subtle change that touches every sm:* utility across the codebase; a visual regression on desktop 1280 is still passing but any component that used to hide/show content at 640 now flips at 600, which could nudge tablet-portrait layouts slightly. No functional issues seen, but worth noting for QA rounds.

AppShell.jsx uses `header-menu-dropdown` as the single dropdown for both search + notifs + logout — verified on-screen at 375 (x=71, width=288), but if the dropdown gains 2+ more items it could exceed viewport height. Fine for now.

Pipeline kanban horizontal scroll pattern (overflow-x-auto flex gap-3) is exactly right — 2388px of kanban content scrolls inside a 728px container without producing page-level overflow. Same pattern should be reused for any future wide table.

PdfPreviewModal.jsx mobile fallback did not render in Playwright (automation UA doesn't match mobile regex) — the modal correctly falls through to the iframe with Download + New-tab buttons in the header, so the mobile user always has an exit. Consider adding a viewport-width check (window.innerWidth < 600) in addition to UA sniffing so the fallback also engages on narrow desktops.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (51/51 route-viewport overflow, 6/6 mobile-modal flows, 3/3 tablet, 1/1 desktop)

test_credentials: admin@projexino.com / Projexino@2026 — verified login via [data-testid=login-email]+[data-testid=login-password]+[data-testid=login-submit]; token stored in localStorage keys pj_token + pj_role.
seed_data_creation: No data created. Every modal opened was closed without submit (playbook builder, documents share, outreach email-blast, PDF preview, header menu, notifications sheet).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full responsive contract verified: Mobile 0-599 / Tablet 600-1023 / Desktop 1024+. Tailwind screens: sm=600, md=768, lg=1024, xl=1280. Zero horizontal overflow on 17 routes x 3 viewports (375, 800, 1280). Verified modals at 375px: pb-builder-modal + pb-save + pb-live-preview; doc-share-modal + doc-share-to + doc-share-send; email-blast-modal + blast-template + blast-send; pdf-preview-modal + Download; header-menu-dropdown fully on-screen; app-notif-sheet as bottom sheet. Verified tablet 800px: HR tab pills wrap, pipeline kanban inner-scroll (scrollWidth 2388 within 728 clientWidth) with page docW=800, settings inputs render OK. Route redirects: /app/hr-letters -> /app/hr, /app/email-templates -> /app/outreach — expected, launchpad routes are the canonical entry. Escape key doesn't universally close all modals but that's out-of-scope for a responsive audit (iter71/72 already verified explicit close buttons work). No console errors on any page. Gmail actions were NOT triggered (send buttons not clicked) — no OAuth 400s recorded. All modals opened were closed without saving. No PDF was downloaded, no lead was created, no letter was written.
rca_of_the_issue: No issues found. The Iter73 responsive hardening is comprehensive and correct: (a) tailwind screens contract lines up with the 0-599 / 600-1023 / 1024+ breakpoints, (b) .portal-scope main has overflow-x:hidden so wide tables/kanbans scroll only inside their overflow-x-auto wrappers, (c) high z-index modals (z-[60..75]) get bottom-sheet treatment under 600px, (d) media elements clamp to max-width:100%, (e) header dropdown + notification sheet are viewport-safe, (f) email shell table is fluid width:100% max-width:600px, (g) PDF preview falls back to Download/New-tab buttons on mobile. Ready to ship.
